---
title: "Linear Algebra"
description: "Core linear algebra for research-facing work in CS, AI, and engineering."
page-type: module-overview
status: published
update-status: current
level: foundation
topic: linear-algebra
prerequisites:
  - /start-here/index.qmd
learning-goals:
  - "Build geometric and operator intuition."
  - "Connect core matrix ideas to estimation, optimization, and spectral methods."
  - "Use the module as a bridge into advanced theory papers."
applications:
  - machine-learning
  - optimization
  - signal-processing
keywords:
  - linear algebra
  - least squares
  - spectral methods
maintainer: mtuann
last-reviewed: 2026-04-24
source-check-date: 2026-04-24
review-cadence: annual
date-modified: last-modified
draft: false
---

## Why This Module Matters

Linear algebra is one of the most important modules on the whole site. Modern ML, optimization, signal processing, control, and statistics all keep returning to vectors, operators, geometry, and spectra.

::: {.meta-strip}
::: {.meta-box}
**Prerequisites**
Algebra and late single-variable calculus
:::
::: {.meta-box}
**Unlocks**
Multivariable calculus, optimization, numerics, matrix analysis
:::
::: {.meta-box}
**Research Use**
Least squares, SVD, covariance, kernels, spectral arguments
:::
:::

## First Pass Through This Module

1. [Vectors and Linear Combinations](concepts/vectors-and-linear-combinations.qmd)
2. Matrices and linear maps
3. Subspaces, basis, dimension
4. [Orthogonality and Least Squares](concepts/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd)
5. Eigenvalues and diagonalization
6. [SVD and Low-Rank Approximation](concepts/svd-and-low-rank-approximation.qmd)

On a first pass, stay on the concept pages. You should not need proof, application, lab, paper-lab, research, or source-guide pages just to understand the main story of the module.

## Navigation Rule

The global sidebar shows only the linear algebra `spine`:

- the module overview
- the core concept pages in the main study order

The proof, exercise, application, lab, paper-lab, research, and source-guide pages are `deep-dive companions`. They stay linked from this module page and the topic concept pages, rather than all appearing in the main sidebar at once.

## Go Deeper By Topic

### Orthogonality and Least Squares

Start with [Orthogonality and Least Squares](concepts/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd).

If you want more after the main page:

- `Proof`: [Projection Theorem and Normal Equations](proofs/projection-theorem-and-normal-equations.qmd)
- `Application`: [Linear Regression Through Projection](applications/linear-regression-through-projection.qmd)
- `Visual intuition`: [Computation Lab: Projection Geometry and Regression Residuals](labs/orthogonality-and-least-squares-geometry.qmd)

### SVD and Low-Rank Approximation

Start with [SVD and Low-Rank Approximation](concepts/svd-and-low-rank-approximation.qmd).

If you want more after the main page:

- `Proof`: [Eckart-Young and Truncated SVD](proofs/eckart-young-and-truncated-svd.qmd)
- `Application`: [PCA Through SVD](applications/pca-through-svd.qmd)
- `Visual intuition`: [Computation Lab: Rank-1 Approximation and PCA Geometry](labs/rank-one-approximation-and-pca-geometry.qmd)

## Optional Deep Dives After First Pass

After the first pass, use these pages when you want research-facing depth rather than basic understanding:

- [Paper Lab: Randomized Sketching for Least Squares](paper-lab/randomized-sketching-for-least-squares.qmd)
- [Research Direction: Least Squares, Sketching, and Overparameterized Regression](research/least-squares-sketching-and-overparameterized-regression.qmd)
- [Source Guide: Orthogonality and Least Squares](sources/orthogonality-and-least-squares.qmd)
- [Paper Lab: Finding Structure with Randomness](paper-lab/finding-structure-with-randomness.qmd)
- [Research Direction: Low-Rank Structure from Randomized Algorithms to Model Adaptation](research/low-rank-structure-from-randomized-algorithms-to-model-adaptation.qmd)
- [Source Guide: SVD and Low-Rank Approximation](sources/svd-and-low-rank-approximation.qmd)

## What You Want By The End

- geometric intuition
- symbolic fluency
- operator viewpoint
- ability to recognize where a paper is really using spectral structure

## Application Door

If you want a first application target, start with `least squares -> SVD -> PCA -> low-rank approximation`. That chain alone will pay off again and again.

## Sources and Further Reading

- [MIT 18.06SC Linear Algebra resource index](https://ocw.mit.edu/courses/18-06sc-linear-algebra-fall-2011/pages/resource-index/) - `First pass` - strong official module spine. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Stanford Math 51](https://web.stanford.edu/class/math51/) - `First pass` - current applied framing for the linear algebra side of the module. Checked `2026-04-24`.
- [Hefferon, Linear Algebra](https://hefferon.net/linearalgebra/) - `Second pass` - proof-and-exercise depth for self-study. Checked `2026-04-24`.
